rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical 1,4-selective dialkoxylation of 1,3-dienes with alcohols in an undivided cell to give (E)-1,4-diether alkenes
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of methoxide (MeO-) to methoxy radical (MeO•); subsequent anodic oxidation of allyl/benzyl radical intermediate II to allyl cation III

key_intermediates.name: primary allyl radical I from MeO• addition to terminal alkene of diene
allyl/benzyl cation III (after one-electron oxidation of II)

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes MeO- to MeO•, which adds to the terminal carbon of the 1,3-diene to give an allyl radical that rearranges to the more stable (E)-benzylic radical II; II is further oxidized at the anode to allyl cation III, which is trapped by MeOH/MeO- to deliver the 1,4-diether (path A, favored over direct radical-radical coupling of II with MeO•, path B).
rate_determining_step: Anodic ionization of radical II to cation III (computed ionization energy 107.21 kcal/mol); overall radical addition/rearrangement step is strongly exergonic (-31.11 kcal/mol)

probe_reagents.name: BHT (added at t=0, 3.0 eq)
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction completely inhibited; BHT-OMe adduct isolated in 39% yield, confirming generation of MeO• radical

probe_reagents.name: BHT (added after 2 h, 3.0 eq)
probe_reagents.observation: Mono-methoxylation BHT-trapped radical intermediate detected by HRMS, supporting carbon radical intermediate II

notes: Two pathways considered: path A (oxidation of radical II to cation III, then MeO- capture; methoxylation step exergonic by 58.28 kcal/mol, favored) vs path B (direct radical-radical coupling of II with MeO•). Path A favored thermodynamically and by suppression of homocoupling via steric effects. DFT shows (E)-II more stable than (Z)-II by 3.82 kcal/mol and 1,4-radical II more stable than 1,2-radical II' by 2.80 kcal/mol in MeOH complex state, rationalizing E- and 1,4-selectivity.
